# Arodeploy

> Arodeploy is a bare-metal deployment platform built to lower hosting costs and make low-level server infrastructure accessible without sacrificing control. It provides a scripting layer (aro-script) that lets anyone define, deploy, and manage any server setup on bare-metal hardware.

Arodeploy was created out of frustration with the cost of managed hosting. The project prioritises full configuration control, composable scripting, and reusable templates — targeting both framework developers (e.g. Next.js) and infrastructure engineers.

Key notes:

- Arodeploy is self-hosted and runs on bare-metal hardware, not managed cloud services.
- The aro-script format is the core abstraction: readable, composable, shareable, and vendor-neutral.
- The platform exposes live service endpoints at api.arodeploy.com and console.arodeploy.com.
- Questions can be directed to aaron.gotthardsson@gmail.com.

## Services

- [API](https://api.arodeploy.com): The Arodeploy backend API.
- [Console](https://console.arodeploy.com): The Arodeploy web console for managing deployments.

## Site

- [Landing page](https://arodeploy.com): Project overview, service status, and contact information.
